terms.definition: Given class y, features x₁,…,x_d factorize: P(x|y) = ∏ᵢ P(xᵢ|y). The defining simplification of naive Bayes classifiers.
Informal name for conditional feature independence given the label; "naive" because real features often interact, but the product form is computationally tractable.
Replacing joint P(x|y) with a product of marginal per-feature terms P(xᵢ|y), reducing parameters from exponential to linear in feature count.
P(x|y): the probability (or density) of observing features x when the true class is y; in naive Bayes this is the product ∏ᵢ P(xᵢ|y).
A model that specifies P(x|y) and P(y), then derives P(y|x) via Bayes' theorem; naive Bayes is generative because it models how data arises per class.
log P(y) + Σᵢ log P(xᵢ|y): numerically stable score used for argmax classification; equivalent to P(y|x) up to normalization.
